greetings.context: Krishi Sakhi is an AI farming assistant specifically designed for Kerala farmers. It provides personalized advice based on local conditions, crops, and farming practices.
responses: Hello! I'm Krishi Sakhi, your AI farming assistant for Kerala. I specialize in helping farmers with crop management, weather advice, and agricultural best practices. How can I help you today?
Namaste! Welcome to Krishi Sakhi. I'm here to provide expert farming guidance tailored to Kerala's unique agricultural conditions. What farming questions do you have?

Greetings, farmer friend! I'm Krishi Sakhi, your dedicated agricultural AI assistant. I can help with everything from crop selection to pest management in Kerala's climate. What would you like to know?

rice.cultivation: Rice thrives in Kerala's monsoon climate. Plant during monsoon onset for best results. Requires well-prepared puddled fields with standing water.
rice.fertilizer: Apply NPK 90:45:45 kg per hectare along with 5 tons of organic manure. Split application - 50% nitrogen as basal, 25% at tillering, 25% at panicle initiation.
rice.diseases: Common issues include blast disease, brown spot, and sheath blight. Use Tricyclazole for blast control and maintain proper field hygiene.
rice.yield: Expected yield is 4-6 tons per hectare with proper management
rice.market_price: Current price ranges from ₹2,800 to ₹3,200 per quintal depending on quality and variety

coconut.cultivation: Coconut is Kerala's pride crop. Plant during pre-monsoon (May-June) or post-monsoon (September-October) for best establishment.
coconut.fertilizer: Apply 50kg organic manure, 1.3kg Urea, 2kg Single Super Phosphate, and 2kg Muriate of Potash per palm annually.
coconut.diseases: Root wilt and bud rot are major concerns. Ensure proper drainage and avoid waterlogging. Use Aureofungin for root wilt treatment.
coconut.yield: A mature palm yields 80-120 nuts annually. Dwarf varieties start bearing in 3-4 years, tall varieties in 6-8 years.
coconut.market_price: Fresh coconuts sell for ₹25-40 per nut depending on size and local demand

varieties: Sreekara - suitable for high rainfall areas
pepper.cultivation: Black pepper is Kerala's spice king. Plant during monsoon onset (May-June) with proper support structures. Requires well-drained, organic-rich soil.
pepper.fertilizer: Apply 10kg organic manure annually along with NPK 50:50:100 grams per vine. Split application during pre-monsoon and post-monsoon.
pepper.diseases: Quick wilt is the most serious disease. Prevent through proper drainage and avoid waterlogging. Use Metalaxyl + Mancozeb for treatment.
pepper.yield: A mature vine yields 1-3 kg of dried pepper annually. Commercial production starts from 3rd year.
pepper.market_price: Dried black pepper prices range from ₹500-800 per kg based on quality and market conditions

weather_advice.monsoon: During monsoon, focus on transplanting rice, maintaining drainage in coconut gardens, and supporting pepper vines. Monitor for fungal diseases due to high humidity.
weather_advice.summer: In summer months, ensure adequate irrigation, mulching around coconut palms, and prepare land for next season. This is harvest time for many crops.
weather_advice.winter: Winter is ideal for vegetable cultivation, harvesting of rice (Mundakan season), and maintenance work in perennial crops. Good time for organic manure application.

pm_kisan.name: PM-KISAN (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi)
pm_kisan.description: Direct income support scheme providing ₹6,000 per year to small and marginal farmers in three installments of ₹2,000 each.
pm_kisan.eligibility: Farmers with combined land holding up to 2 hectares

pmfby.name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana
pmfby.description: Comprehensive crop insurance scheme protecting farmers against crop losses due to natural calamities, pests, and diseases.
pmfby.premium: Farmers pay only 2% for Kharif, 1.5% for Rabi, and 5% for horticulture crops

kcc.name: Kisan Credit Card
kcc.description: Credit facility for farmers to meet agricultural expenses with flexible repayment and low interest rates.
kcc.interest_rate: 7% for timely repayment, subsidized rates available

market_information.price_factors: Market prices depend on quality, season, demand-supply situation, and government policies. Always check local market rates before selling.
market_information.marketing_tips: Grade your produce properly, time your sales during peak demand, explore direct marketing options, and maintain post-harvest quality.
market_information.price_trends: Rice prices are generally stable throughout the year. Coconut prices fluctuate based on oil industry demand. Spice prices are more volatile and dependent on export markets.

pest_disease_management.integrated_approach: Use IPM (Integrated Pest Management) combining cultural, biological, and chemical methods. Always prefer organic and biological controls first.
pest_disease_management.common_pests: Rice: Brown planthopper, stem borer. Coconut: Rhinoceros beetle, red palm weevil. Pepper: Thrips, mites, nematodes.
pest_disease_management.organic_solutions: Neem oil, garlic-chili extract, pheromone traps, beneficial insects, and proper crop rotation help control pests naturally.

soil_management.kerala_soils: Kerala has diverse soils - laterite in midland, alluvial in lowland, and forest soils in highlands. Each requires specific management.
soil_management.soil_health: Regular soil testing every 2-3 years helps optimize fertilizer use. Maintain soil pH between 6.0-7.5 for most crops.
soil_management.organic_matter: Add organic matter regularly through crop residues, green manuring, and compost. This improves soil structure and water retention.

irrigation_water.water_management: Kerala receives good rainfall but water conservation is important. Use drip irrigation, mulching, and rainwater harvesting.
irrigation_water.irrigation_scheduling: Water rice fields continuously but maintain 2-3 cm water depth. For coconut, deep watering once a week is better than frequent light watering.
irrigation_water.water_quality: Use clean water for irrigation. Avoid saline or polluted water which can harm crops and soil health.
